title: Would I go back to school? - Request

transcript.text: Hello, hello, video request. I almost missed this one because he sent it to me, a private email, and I don't usually remember them that way, but it's better to do them on my homepage on YouTube.
So this one's from Don Jovi. I heard Newt Gingrich say about unemployment that if only we'll take 700 days to get a degree. Would you spend 700 days learning a skill for a career at your age?
If you did it through a college, you would have a lot of the basic skills classes, so it wouldn't even be 700 days. Would you do it? Why or why not?
Yeah, dude, it's never too late to stop learning. I mean, take me. He's asking me personally. Well, I'm actually already doing that. I'm studying history all day, every day.
And it's taken a lot more than 700 days. It's taken thousands and thousands of days.
So, I mean, yeah, if I wasn't doing the history, I'd be learning something else.

Yeah, that was a good point that Newt Gingrich made. If the economy is having problems, you know, one thing that causes the economy to have problems is when there's an oversaturation of a certain market where all these people are competing for a certain, you know, in a certain industry.
And so then they're not working as much and not making as much money. And then but, you know, in America, there's there's other industries that are hiring.
And so people should definitely find out which industries are hiring and then and then and then just learn how to do them.
And then you can get that job and you're in your in your off. Yeah, country is going to do a lot better if people can figure out what jobs are needed.
And then and then they can fill those jobs. That's what will kickstart the economy better than anything.
So. So, yeah, hell, yeah. And it's never too late. I mean, most of these high paying, you know, high, high skills jobs, they're not labor jobs.
So it's stuff that old people can do and old people's brains work until the day you die. Most people.
And so it's never too late, you know, never, never, never too late.
So, yeah, I mean, what else what is else is there to do anyways, if you're if you're bored with your life, one of the most exciting things you can do is learn.
And, you know, so you would learn it just to learn it, you know, I would think.
And then you could make money with it. If you don't like your job, yeah, get another job and learn how to do that other job.
Now, now, college, I wouldn't I personally, I don't think people should try to do it through colleges.
They should try to if they can do it without colleges, it's better.
I mean, there's unfortunately, there's a lot of draconian laws that say that if you're going to get a job, you have to have a degree.
And also with the unions, if you have a degree, they have to pay you more.
And that's one reason that people like to get that degree so that they get to be paid more.
But then that makes it harder to get hired because people don't like hiring people who they have to pay more.
But I would I think it's I think another good thing for the economy is if people can learn how to teach themselves things.

I would love to. In fact, I mean, pretty much planning on it because I plan on being I mean, I would like to think that I'll be around for a long enough that I'll have the time to learn like law, for example.
I'd like to learn law, but I would never go to a law school.
It's too expensive. And you can learn all that stuff yourself.
And if you can just get your hands on the on the tests, just pass the bar, then you can call yourself a lawyer, you know, just pass that bar.
And after studying yourself and and then show other people how you did it and let as many people as you can know that you did it and how you did it to inspire other people to do it.
And people learning stuff themselves is that's the best way to learn when you're guiding yourself.

You know, I think education is great and schools can do a good job of kind of helping guide people and motivate people to to like finish certain things by a certain date so that they don't so that they're not halfway done with everything.
But people should still be able to, you know, you know, you know, be as much in control of their of what they learn, you know, as they can.
And people who can learn themselves are going to be better lawyer, you know, somebody who learned law themselves and pass the bar.
I bet you they do a better job on that bar than the person who who went to Harvard Law.
The person who went to Harvard Law would have a little bit would have more practice actually debating people in real life.
But you could you could pick that.
You could learn how to debate pretty quickly.
You know, if you know your stuff, you don't really need.
And you could, you know, you could set up people who educate themselves could just set up little little societies of people, you know, in a world with all the interconnectedness these days.

People can there's always going to be other people like you and if you can motivate other people to be like you, you can set up little chat rooms where people can debate, do legal debates with people.
And, yeah, and that goes with anything, you know, medicine, they should set up the government should set up just like places like libraries.
They should expand libraries.
I should do a video about the future of libraries where you can study even medical stuff where they where there's rooms where there's microscopes and telescopes and even rooms of like, you know,
One day out of a month, there's a cadaver, people can go in there and check out and learn themselves and become a become a doctor yourself, not even go into a medical school, just become a doctor.
And and that's the way I would do it.
But, you know, a lot of the a lot of the skills with where they're hiring now is computer skills, different computer languages, which you can learn yourself as well.
And that would be a good thing for people to do.
So, yeah, I mean, it's never too late.
Never. I don't even understand the concept of why somebody would think it's too late.
Like, why? Like you forgot how to do homework or something, you know?
Yeah, people don't people don't forget how to do homework.
They don't get dumber as they get older.
They get smarter. You do better at school if you get if you take a few years off and then go back to it.
So now what would now why or why not?
Why? Well, if you're doing a job that's fresh and you're and and, you know, is exciting, then you're going to be happier.
And if you're getting doing a job that pays more because it's more in demand, then you don't have to work as much.
And then you'll be able to afford to go, you know, travel to Rome and maybe, you know, you could score a girlfriend and have kids and all that stuff.
Quality of better quality of life.
So, yeah, go for it.
That's what I do for sure.
Right now I'm becoming a master historian of all on my own.
And that'll make me money.
That'll be my job.
I made four dollars yesterday on YouTube, but if I make once I make my new channel with all my history videos with my timeline, I can make really good videos and make money with that.
And then maybe you get to get to be a tutor for history.
You know, I could be a history teacher.
There's all kinds of kinds of possibilities with that.
I could be a consultant on a movie set for historical movie or something like that.
Write books, make money on my timeline.
There's all kinds of ways.
So just do what you want to do.
Do whatever you're excited about and then you're not even thinking about money because you're too busy doing the thing you're excited about to get good at it.
And then eventually it'll make you money.
So go for it.
Peace out.
